LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SPECTRUM BRANDS, INC.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. CCP 335.1 [2 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS] BARS PLAINTIFF'S ACTION. UNDER 11 USC 362(C)(2), THE BANKRUPTCY STAY WAS LIFTED WHEN THE BANKRUPTCY COURT ISSUED THE REORGANIZATION/DISCHARGE ORDER ON JULY 15, 2009. PURSUANT TO 11 USC 108(C), PLAINTIFF HAD UNTIL " 30 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF THE TERMINATION OR EXPIRATION OF THE STAY" TO FILE HER ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ADMITS THAT SHE HAD NOTICE THAT THE STAY WAS LIFTED BY DECEMBER 1 OR 4 OF 2009. THE COURT CAN TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THAT AFFIDAVIT. SEE DEL E. WEBB CORP. V. STRUCTURAL MATERIALS CO. (1981) 123 CAL.APP.3D 593, 604-605 ("THE COURT WILL TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF RECORDS SUCH AS ADMISSIONS, ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES, AFFIDAVITS, AND THE LIKE, WHEN CONSIDERING A DEMURRER, ONLY WHERE THEY CONTAIN STATEMENTS OF THE PLAINTIFF OR HIS AGENT WHICH ARE INCONSISTENT WITH THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE PLEADING BEFORE THE COURT.") PLAINTIFF FILED THIS UNTIMELY ACTION MORE THAN 30 DAYS LATER ON JANUARY 29, 2010. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF JUDGMENT.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT DEFENDANT SPECTRUM BRANDS, INC. ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS SPECTRUM JUNGLE LAB, CORP. TAKE NOTHING FROM PLAINTIFF PEDROIA, SANDRA DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO COSTS AS DETERMINED BY PROOF PURSUANT TO RULE OF COURT 3.1700 SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL DID NOT CONTEST THE TENTATIVE RULNG. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL OBJECTED TO MR. HENNING MAKING A SPECIAL APPEARANCE FOR THE PLAINTIFF. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SANDRA PEDROIA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER GRANTING JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. THE COURT LACKS THE POWER TO GRANT RECONSIDERATION BECAUSE JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED ON FEBRUARY 20, 2014. SEE RAMON V. AEROSPACE CORP. (1996) 50 CAL.APP.4TH 1233, 1236; APRI INS. CO. V. SUP. CRT. (1999) 76 CAL.APP.4TH 176, 181-182. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2014 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Melissa L. Grant, Esq. of Santana & Hart, Tel. 415.777.1308 x16054, for defendant and prevailing party;
John F. Henning III, Esq. of the Law Office of John F. Henning III, Tel. 415.758.1382, appearing specially for plaintiff and moving party.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel did not contest the tentative rulng. Defendant's counsel objected to Mr. Henning making a special appearance for the plaintiff. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SANDRA PEDROIA'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Judgment On The Pleadings is denied. The Court lacks the power to grant reconsideration because judgment was entered on February 20, 2014. See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236; APRI Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 176, 181-182. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-03-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On May-07-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jan-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MELISSA GRANT, ESQ., 415.777.1308, FOR DEFENDANT SPECTRUM BRANDS, INC.
JOHN F. HENNING II, ESQ., 415.398.5000; No other appearances.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SPECTRUM BRANDS, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend. CCP 335.1 [2 year statute of limitations] bars plaintiff's action. Under 11 USC 362(c)(2), the bankruptcy stay was lifted when the bankruptcy court issued the reorganization/discharge order on July 15, 2009. Pursuant to 11 USC 108(c), plaintiff had until " 30 days after notice of the termination or expiration of the stay" to file her action. Plaintiff's declaration in opposition to the motion for summary judgment admits that she had notice that the stay was lifted by December 1 or 4 of 2009. The Court can take judicial notice of that affidavit. See Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604-605 ("The court will take judicial notice of records such as admissions, answers to interrogatories, affidavits, and the like, when considering a demurrer, only where they contain statements of the plaintiff or his agent which are inconsistent with the allegations of the pleading before the court.") Plaintiff filed this untimely action more than 30 days later on January 29, 2010. Order signed in open court. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of judgment.) Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
